scenarios.opening_query: The state took my nephew's college fund money during his arrest and the family wants it back.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Forfeiture || General Litigation > Actions Against Police
scenarios.hidden_fact: The charges were dropped AND the forfeiture case dismissed months ago — the police simply haven't returned the $12,000, and suing the department for the wrongful retention is what's left.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Charges dropped, forfeiture dismissed months ago — the police just haven't returned the $12,000. Suing over the retention is what's left.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the forfeiture case is over leaving a wrongful-retention claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The civil forfeiture case is active — the state's claim notice gives us 30 days to contest it, and that's the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The forfeiture case is active — 30 days to contest per the notice.
scenarios.vetting_note: Case-over-but-property-held posture vs live forfeiture contest.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been clean for nine years and my old drug case still poisons every background check.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Expunction || Criminal Law > Clemency
scenarios.hidden_fact: The conviction is a federal one — no expungement exists for it; a presidential pardon application is the only path, and there's a process for it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a federal conviction — no expungement exists. The pardon application is the only path.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the conviction is federal requiring clemency
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a state conviction that qualifies for set-aside now — the petition just needs filing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a state conviction that qualifies now — the petition just needs filing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Federal-vs-state jurisdiction selects clemency vs expungement.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's sentence was supposed to end this spring but the prison says he owes more time.
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Post Conviction/Appeals || General Litigation > Civil Matters for Inmates
scenarios.hidden_fact: The extra time is DOC's own recalculation of good-time credits — an agency computation error, challenged through habeas/administrative channels against the department, not the sentence itself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's DOC's recalculation of good-time credits — an agency error to challenge against the department, not the sentence.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is agency credit math or the judicial sentence
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The judgment itself was amended at a hearing we didn't know about — attacking that amended sentence is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The judgment itself got amended at a hearing we never knew about — that's what needs attacking.

scenarios.opening_query: I was the getaway driver in name only — I had no idea what they were doing in that store.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: One of them showed a gun inside — so I'm indicted for Robbery I under accomplice liability, Measure 11 minimums and all.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: One of them showed a gun inside — so my indictment is Robbery I as an accomplice, Measure 11 and all.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a weapon elevated everyone's charges
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No weapon — it was shoplifting that became felony theft by the amount, and my count is trailing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No weapon — shoplifting that hit felony theft by amount, and mine's the trailing count.
scenarios.vetting_note: Accomplice exposure rides the principal's conduct facts.

scenarios.opening_query: My conviction is from 1998 and I've been told different things about whether anything can be done.
scenarios.final: Criminal Law > Relief From Sex Offender Registration
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the registration that rules my life — housing, work, everything; the statute now allows relief petitions for my tier after 20 years clean, and I qualify.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The registration rules my life — and the statute now allows relief petitions for my tier after 20 clean years. I qualify.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether registration relief eligibility is the operative remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No registration involved — it's an old theft conviction, and the set-aside statute finally covers it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No registration — it's an old theft conviction the set-aside statute now covers.

scenarios.opening_query: The DA offered my son a deal and his public defender says take it, but something feels wrong to us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || International Law > Deportation
scenarios.hidden_fact: My son is a green-card holder — the offered plea is an aggravated felony for immigration purposes, and accepting it means near-certain removal; crimmigration counsel has to vet the deal first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's a green-card holder — that plea is an aggravated felony for immigration, near-certain removal. Crimmigration counsel has to vet it first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether immigration consequences control the plea decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's a citizen — the worry is just whether the deal is actually good, and a second opinion on the defense is what we want.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's a citizen — we just want a second opinion on whether the deal's good.
scenarios.vetting_note: Padilla-consult posture before plea; citizenship is the fact.
